evidence: extract_bash_targets() applies redirect regex patterns to the full segment string without stripping quoted regions. '-> in-progress' inside a Python string matched the '>>' pattern, yielding 'in-progress' as a write target. Actual write was to an allowlisted .claude/backlogs/ path. False positives affect any command whose string data contains '>', '>>', 'tee', 'cp', 'mv', 'rm', 'touch', or 'mkdir' tokens.
proposed_handoff: Make extract_bash_targets() quote-aware by stripping single-quoted and double-quoted regions before applying redirect regexes. Add regression tests for heredoc body with '->', single/double-quoted strings with '> path', and confirm real redirects still detected.
